page: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The memory configuration provides 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a 22 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier capabilities in memory subsystem performance and multi-core execution. The instance achieves top-tier status in large-block memory bandwidth, reaching 404.36 GB/sec for read-only and 201.11 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23531.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 47285.97, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Additionally, static web serving throughput is highly efficient at 103.71 GB/sec. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.12xlarge leverages the arm64 Graviton architecture to deliver high memory density and memory bandwidth per resource unit. This makes it highly competitive for workloads that scale horizontally across physical cores without hyperthreading overhead. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. The absence of AVX/AMX instruction sets also limits its suitability for specialized x86 vector acceleration.

This instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, in-memory caches like Redis, and high-throughput web serving. It is also well-suited for software compilation tasks and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation, where memory bandwidth is a primary bottleneck.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core), 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds of 404.36 GB/sec and write speeds of 201.11 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core execution in database operations and software compilation. Qualitatively, its custom arm64 architecture provides excellent resource density and memory throughput relative to its hardware class, though the lack of local storage and x86 vector extensions represent notable tradeoffs. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 48 Graviton cores and 384 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores (arm64)
Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching 404.36 GB/sec read and 201.11 GB/sec write
Baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
